name: Old Fashioned English Treacle Tart
recipeIngredient: 5 ounces rich short crust pastry, see note
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the syrup in a saucepan with the bread crumbs, lemon rind, and juice.
2. Heat gently until just melted. Allow to cool.
3. Roll out the dough and use it to line an 8-inch pie plate.
4. Pour in the syrup mixture.
5. Roll out any remaining dough trimmings and cut into long, narrow strips.
6. Arrange the strips in a lattice pattern over the filling.
7. Bake in a moderately hot oven (400°F) for 25 to 30 minutes, or until the pastry is slightly browned.
8. Serve hot or cold.
a. Golden Syrup, or treacle may be substituted for light molasses or honey.
b. You may substitute the pastry with an unbaked pie crust from your local bakery.
